title: cytisanthus hassertianus
intro: Cytisanthus hassertianus, also known as Hassert's Cytisus, is a rare, flowering plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is a shrubby perennial with yellow flowers, but it is known to be toxic and should be handled with care. The plant contains cytisine, a pyridine alkaloid with medicinal properties, which has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Cytisanthus hassertianus has been used in herbal preparations to help alleviate coughs and promote expectoration, but it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
